meta.name: PRMQ Prospective and Retrospective Memory Questionnaire
meta.description: The PRMQ is a 16-item self-report measure that assesses the frequency of everyday memory failures. It distinguishes between prospective memory (remembering to do things in the future) and retrospective memory (recalling past events and information).
meta.author: Smith et al. (2000)
meta.reference: Smith G, Della Sala S, Logie RH, Maylor EA. Prospective and retrospective memory in normal ageing and dementia: a questionnaire study. Mem Cognit. 2000;28(6):881-889.
meta.instruction: The following questions are about the kinds of memory mistakes that everyone makes from time to time. Please indicate how often each of these things happens to you.

questions.text: Do you decide to do something in a few minutes' time and then forget to do it?
Do you fail to recognize a place you have visited before?
Do you fail to do something you were supposed to do a few minutes later even though it's there in front of you?
Do you forget something that you were told a few minutes before?
Do you forget appointments if you are not prompted by someone else or by a reminder?
Do you fail to recognize a character in a radio or television show from scene to scene?
Do you forget to buy something you planned to buy, like a birthday card, even when you see the shop?
Do you fail to recall things that have happened to you in the last few days?
Do you repeat the same story to the same person on different occasions?
Do you intend to take something with you but minutes later leave it behind?
Do you mislay something that you have just put down, like a magazine or glasses?
Do you fail to mention or give something to a visitor that you were asked to pass on?
Do you look at something without realizing you have seen it moments before?
If you tried to contact a friend or relative who was out, would you forget to try again later?
Do you forget what you watched on television the previous day?
Do you forget to tell someone something you had meant to mention a few minutes ago?

ranges.label: Good Memory
ranges.description: Your overall memory performance appears to be good, with infrequent memory failures in daily life.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current memory strategies
Maintain healthy lifestyle habits that support cognitive function

ranges.label: Average Memory
ranges.description: Your memory performance is within the average range. You experience occasional memory lapses that are common in daily life.
suggestions: Use external aids like calendars and reminder apps for important tasks
Establish consistent routines to support prospective memory
Stay mentally active through reading, puzzles, or learning new skills
Ensure adequate sleep, as it is critical for memory consolidation

ranges.label: Poor Memory
ranges.description: Your score suggests you experience memory failures more frequently than average. This may affect daily functioning.
suggestions: Implement systematic reminder strategies (phone alarms, sticky notes, to-do lists)
Minimize distractions when trying to remember important information
Ensure adequate sleep, as sleep is critical for memory consolidation
Consider whether stress or multitasking may be contributing to memory lapses

ranges.label: Very Poor Memory
ranges.description: Your score indicates frequent memory failures that may significantly impact daily life.
suggestions: Consider consulting a healthcare professional for a thorough memory assessment
Use multiple external memory aids consistently
Simplify routines and reduce cognitive load where possible
Discuss with a professional whether underlying factors may be involved

prospective.label: Good Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You generally remember to carry out planned actions and future intentions.

prospective.label: Average Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You occasionally forget to carry out planned actions, which is within the normal range.

prospective.label: Poor Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You frequently forget to carry out intended actions. Consider using external reminders.

prospective.label: Very Poor Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You very frequently forget planned actions. Systematic use of external aids is strongly recommended.

retrospective.label: Good Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You generally recall past events and previously learned information well.

retrospective.label: Average Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You occasionally have difficulty recalling past events, which is within the normal range.

retrospective.label: Poor Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You frequently have difficulty remembering past events and information.

retrospective.label: Very Poor Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You very frequently have difficulty recalling past events. Consider seeking professional assessment.
